text:
Re: kern/114213: [ata] optical drive not detected in the 6.x series of FreeBSD/i386 (regression)

YES, it is still an issue with FreeBSD 6.3.
I just tested 6.3 on the very same two computers (same hw config) my original report was based on.

1., My old PIII/800 desktop is a Slot-1 Soltek SL67-EV1/FV1 based system with 3x256MB SD-RAM, a Samsung 160GB hdd as primary master (no primary slave device), a Pioneer DVD reader as secondary master AND an LG CD-ROM reader as secondary slave.
I booted off of the FreeBSD 6.3-RELEASE Install CD in the DVD reader. The system nicely boots up and the first sysinstall menu is displayed, but the DVD drive did not show up in dmesg (the LG CD-ROM drive did).
This is exactly how 6.2R and 6.1R behaved on this hardware.
When sysinstall showed up, I had to move the boot CD from the DVD reader to the CD-ROM drive and use the FIXIT shell, otherwise FIXIT didn't start either (Please insert CD ...). The verbose boot message below was generated using the FIXIT shell from the CD.

2., The other system is an IBM Netfinity 5000 which has an integrated Adaptec SCSI controller, 5x9GB SCSI hdd, and a single integrated IDE socket which has the old rubbis CD-ROM reader hooked up as primary master.
Just on the side note: due to this single machine I filed more PRs here already than I did alltogether before. I begin to believe that this old machine is programmed to generate FreeBSD incompatibility problems. :-)
Just like with earlier 6.x Install CDs, FreeBSD boots up nicely up to the first sysinstall screen, but dmesg showed no CD-ROM drive, therefore neither installation nor FIXIT works. This also meant that I was unable to save a verbose dmesg using the CD.
To generate the verbose dmesg below I synced the system src on the machine to RELENG_6 today and built world. This updated the installed system on the harddisk to 6.3-STABLE, which is supposed to include everything that is new in 6.3R.
